title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
description: The system stores data in a relational database without proper input validation and sanitization. This creates a vulnerability to SQL injection attacks, allowing attackers to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to data breaches, data modification, or even complete system takeover.
attack_vector: Injecting malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes, data entry forms) to alter database queries.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ input validation and sanitization techniques to filter out malicious characters and code.
Regularly update database software to patch known vulnerabilities.
